hero_subtitle: SFTY consolidates multi-provider blockchain analytics into a unified compliance workflow that preserves audit evidence and supports defensible risk decisions for VASPs.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Audit evidence becomes harder to preserve
problem_description: Blockchain analytics outputs remain isolated across multiple providers, creating gaps in case history and decision traceability. Compliance teams struggle to maintain consistent audit trails and defensible evidence around blockchain risk reviews and related actions.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY aggregates blockchain analytics results, normalizes provider outputs, and links review actions with decision logs to create a unified, auditable evidence trail for blockchain risk workflows.
workflow_title: From provider outputs to audit evidence
workflow_description: SFTY structures blockchain analytics data by normalizing provider results, routing reviews, opening cases, recording decisions and preserving evidence for regulatory audit readiness.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond provider outputs. Structured audit evidence
why_sfty_description: SFTY provides digital asset compliance teams a unified layer that preserves blockchain analytics reviews, links provider outputs to cases and secures audit-ready evidence for operational control.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASP operations that require rigorous audit evidence, provider-neutral orchestration and enterprise deployment for clear compliance and bank due diligence workflows.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY preserve audit evidence for blockchain reviews
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how blockchain analytics results become case evidence
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how provider outputs are normalized across blockchain vendors
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Explain how audit trails support VASP compliance workflows
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for VASP compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides VASP compliance teams a connected layer to connect providers, structure workflows, preserve evidence and manage cases across blockchain analytics, onboarding, KYT, Travel Rule and reporting.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle blockchain analytics workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY normalizes multiple blockchain analytics outputs, applies risk rules, routes reviews, opens cases and links evidence to support structured, auditable blockchain risk workflows within one platform.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs and supporting blockchain analytics evidence, supporting review readiness and defensible audit trails for compliance investigations and supervisory reviews.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate existing compliance providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects outputs from KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule, blockchain analytics and screening providers into a single compliance operating workflow without requiring provider replacement.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace blockchain analytics providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above blockchain analytics and other providers, orchestrating their outputs into structured cases and workflows rather than replacing underlying analytics tools.
final_description: Review how SFTY connects blockchain analytics outputs into structured cases, decisions and audit evidence for VASP compliance operations.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
